EditInstructions.en-us: Configure a RAD Admin Table Column.

DropdownValues.*:
:Automatic
number:Number
date:Date
date-time:Date and time
time:Time
boolean:Boolean
progress: Progress

Notes.*: Use this to reference this view / table from other places.

Notes.en-us:
Choose what data to manage. You have three options:

  1. Only use type name. Radmin will then retrieve all items of this type, and if you create a new item, it will be of this type.
  2. Only use query. Radmin will then retrieve the query and allow editing. Create new not be possible since the type is not obvious.
  3. Use both type name and query. Radmin will then retrieve the query, and also allow create-new of the type you specify.
The content type name - will be used to retrieve and/or create new data.
Name of a query to retrieve - if you don't just want to admin all of the specified content-type.
You can just show all columns using automatic behavior, or configure each column manually.
Automatically show all columns which were not specifically configured.

TitleFalse.en-us: hide columns which are not configured

TitleTrue.en-us: show all remaining columns which were not configured

Notes.en-us: Default columns sort and optional direction, CSV like "Title" or "Title,Age" or "Title:desc,Age:asc"
This is for advanced configuration, such as custom JS to adjust how the table / columns will be.
Provide the path to your customizers.js dist output from the base of your app (so everything after: https://website.com/Portals/0/2sxc/AppName/)

By default, RADmin tables can only be viewed by admins. This will allow other users to also see the data. 

Note that this will only affect the configuration. To allow the REST API for data to show the data to other users, update the Content-Type or Query permissions.

TitleFalse.en-us: Prevent anonymous users from viewing this table

TitleTrue.en-us: Allow anonymous / any user to view this table
